success_factors: End-to-end workflow reached finalize with workflow_status=success and no runtime errors/warnings.
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for a Python-first library with high import feasibility (0.93).
Core MCP scaffold files were generated consistently (entrypoint, service, adapter, main, README, requirements).
Service health check passed (healthy over stdio), indicating baseline operability of the generated MCP plugin.
failure_reasons: No hard workflow failure occurred; however, validation depth was weak because original project tests were not executed successfully and MCP test details are empty.
Quality blind spot: generated metadata shows 0 LOC / 0 file size / 0 tool_endpoints despite endpoint list existing, indicating instrumentation/reporting inconsistency.
DeepWiki enrichment failed, reducing external architectural/contextual confidence.

node_performance.generation_time: Code generation completed quickly enough within total 186.84s workflow duration, producing 7 output files; generation metrics are incomplete.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health test passed, but comprehensive functional tests/smoke tests were effectively absent (empty details, 0 execution times), so confidence is limited.
